task: Analyze this social mention for sentiment and engagement opportunity

mention.title: OpenClaw vs Orbit vs Common Room — which one for automating developer relations?
mention.body: We're a 15-person DevTools startup spending $600K/yr on a DevRel team. Tried Orbit but it's mostly community analytics, not actual outreach. Anyone used OpenClaw? How hard is it to set up the multi-agent system with your own knowledge base?
mention.author: curious_dev_42

task: Analyze this social mention for reputation risk

mention.title: OpenClaw — Overhyped and Underdelivering
mention.body: Tried OpenClaw for a week. The Atlas orchestrator hangs on complex tasks, the content agent produces generic blog posts, and the 'competitive intelligence' is basically just web scraping. Save yourself the trouble and hire a real DevRel person or use Common Room for community tracking.
mention.author: disillusioned_hacker

mention.body: Just deployed @DevRelAIAgents with our docs as the knowledge base. 10 specialized agents running our entire DevRel pipeline — GitHub triage, social listening, content creation, competitive intel. Replaced a $500K/yr manual DevRel team in under a week.
mention.author: dev_influencer

mention.title: Migrating from manual DevRel team to OpenClaw — anyone done this?
mention.body: Our Orbit setup only covers community analytics and we still need 3 full-time DevRel people for content, outreach, and competitive intel. Considering OpenClaw as a replacement. Main concern: can the hub-and-spoke architecture handle a repo with 500+ open issues? Any setup guides for retargeting to a custom product?
mention.author: devops_lead
